key_insight: A semantic segmentation model can turn H&E histopathology images into reliable image-level liver cancer diagnoses by identifying the dominant tissue class in each slide.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help pathologists prioritize immunohistochemical marker selection, potentially lowering diagnostic cost and turnaround time while supporting more consistent workflows.
method: Used nnU-Net for semantic segmentation on H&E liver histopathology images.
Applied stain normalization to reduce variability from preparation and scanning differences.
Converted pixel-level segmentation outputs into image-level diagnosis by selecting the dominant predicted class.
evidence_signal: In five-fold cross-validation, the model achieved balanced accuracy of 0.975 for HCC, 0.950 for CCA, and 1.000 for CMA on a dataset of 170 images with pixel-level annotations.
watch_item: The study relies on a relatively small, single-dataset cohort and assumes each image contains only one cancer type, so broader validation across sites and mixed/ambiguous cases is needed.
paper_title: Semantic Segmentation-Driven Image-Level Diagnosis of Liver Cancers in Hematoxylin and Eosin Histopathology Images
